summary: I read both design guides (`guides/backend-guide.md`, `guides/ui-style-guide.md`) and the three Define-phase artifacts under review (`requirements.md`, `open_questions.md`, `discovery_notes.md`), then checked them for guide alignment. The work is deliberately non-technical business requirements — no code, design, or diff — so the implementation-level rules (service/Result interface, thin controllers, no logic in callbacks/jobs, Tailwind type/spacing scale, semantic status colors, Turbo stream targeting, Minitest) have nothing to bind against yet; they become checkable once a design or diff exists downstream. Where the specs do reach guide territory they align: **R4** mandates showing the paused state "using a label and not color alone" (matching the UI guide's status-never-by-color-alone rule), and the discovery notes explicitly treat the guides as binding constraints, mapping each capability onto existing service POROs (`SendBack`, `AnswerQuestions`, `Approve`) rather than callbacks or fat controllers. No requirement pushes the future implementation toward a guide deviation, so I emitted a **pass** verdict with empty findings to `verdict.json`.
